annotations.description: Key-value pairs used to annotate the cluster, providing metadata for identification or organization purposes.

automated_backup_policy.description: Defines the automated backup policy for the AlloyDB cluster, including schedule, retention, and other settings.
automated_backup_policy.type: list(object({backup_window: optional(string),enabled: optional(bool),labels: optional(map(string)),location: optional(string),encryption_config: list(object({kms_key_name: optional(string)})),quantity_based_retention: list(object({count: optional(number)})),time_based_retention: list(object({retention_period: optional(string)})),weekly_schedule: list(object({days_of_week: optional(list(string)),start_times: list(object({hours: optional(number),minutes: optional(number),nanos: optional(number),seconds: optional(number)}))}))}))

cluster_id.description: Unique identifier for the AlloyDB cluster; must be compliant with naming constraints such as being alphanumeric and between 1-63 characters.

cluster_type.description: Specifies the type of AlloyDB cluster, such as 'PRIMARY' for primary clusters or 'SECONDARY' for read replicas, with constraints based on the cluster architecture.

continuous_backup_config.description: Configures continuous backup settings, specifying retention policies and enabling point-in-time recovery for the AlloyDB cluster.

database_version.description: Specifies the database engine version for the AlloyDB cluster, must be a supported version.

deletion_policy.description: Defines the behavior for managing cluster resources upon deletion, such as retaining or removing associated data.

display_name.description: The user-friendly name for the AlloyDB cluster, must be unique within the project and region.

encryption_config.description: Defines the encryption settings for the AlloyDB cluster, specifying the CMEK key to use for data encryption. Requires a valid Google Cloud KMS key.

etag.description: Checksum used to ensure the cluster configuration has not been modified during updates.

initial_user.description: Specifies the initial user account for the AlloyDB cluster, including username and password requirements.

labels.description: Key-value pairs to organize and categorize resources; must be unique within the project and adhere to Google's label format requirements.

location.description: Specifies the regional location for the AlloyDB cluster, must match the region of associated resources.

maintenance_update_policy.description: Specifies the maintenance update policy for the AlloyDB cluster, including the update window and frequency.
maintenance_update_policy.type: list(object({maintenance_windows: list(object({day: string,start_time: list(object({hours: number,minutes: optional(number),nanos: optional(number),seconds: optional(number)}))}))}))

network_config.description: Defines the network configuration for the AlloyDB cluster, specifying the private network to which the cluster is connected. Must be a valid VPC network in the same project and region as the cluster.

psc_config.description: Specifies the private service connect configuration for the cluster, including the consumer and producer service attachments.

restore_backup_source.description: Specifies the backup source to restore the AlloyDB cluster from, including the backup ID and location. Required when creating a cluster from a backup.

restore_continuous_backup_source.description: Specifies the source for restoring a cluster from a continuous backup. Required when creating a cluster from a backup.

secondary_config.description: Configuration settings for a secondary cluster, including replication and read-only properties.

skip_await_major_version_upgrade.description: Determines whether the cluster should skip waiting for a major version upgrade to complete during creation or updates.

subscription_type.description: Specifies the type of subscription for the AlloyDB cluster, such as "on-demand" or "committed use". Ensure it matches the billing plan for your project.

timeouts.description: Defines the time durations for create, update, and delete operations on the AlloyDB cluster, ensuring operations complete within specified limits.
